name: Watermelon
description: A sweet, refreshing summer fruit that loves heat and space to sprawl.
steps: Start seeds indoors 3-4 weeks before last frost.
Plant 1 inch deep in warm soil (70°F+).
Space 60-72 inches apart in hills.
Water deeply and consistently.
Reduce water as fruits ripen for sweeter melons.
Harvest when the tendril nearest the fruit dries and curls.

name: Thyme
description: A hardy, fragrant herb with tiny leaves perfect for seasoning soups, stews, and roasts.
steps: Sow seeds on soil surface; they need light to germinate.
Keep soil moist until germination.
Transplant 12-24 inches apart.
Water sparingly once established.
Harvest sprigs as needed once plant is 6 inches tall.
Prune after flowering to maintain shape.

name: Sage
description: An aromatic, velvety herb used in stuffings, sauces, and teas.
steps: Start seeds indoors 6-8 weeks before last frost.
Plant 0.25 inch deep in well-drained soil.
Transplant 18-24 inches apart.
Water moderately; sage prefers drier conditions.
Harvest leaves before plant flowers for best flavor.
Prune woody stems in early spring.

name: Oregano
description: A robust Mediterranean herb essential for Italian, Greek, and Mexican cooking.
steps: Sprinkle seeds on soil surface; they need light.
Keep moist until germination.
Thin or transplant 12-18 inches apart.
Water when top inch of soil is dry.
Harvest leaves just before flowers open for peak flavor.
Cut stems back to 2 inches in late fall.

name: Kale
description: A nutrient-dense superfood green that thrives in cool weather and tolerates frost.
steps: Sow 0.5 inch deep, 4-6 weeks before last frost.
Thin seedlings to 18-24 inches apart.
Keep soil consistently moist.
Harvest outer leaves when 8-10 inches long.
Frost actually improves flavor.
Mulch heavily for fall/winter growing.

name: Radish
description: One of the fastest-growing vegetables, ready in just 3-4 weeks. Perfect for beginners.
steps: Sow seeds 0.5 inch deep directly outdoors.
Space 2-3 inches apart.
Keep soil evenly moist.
Thin if overcrowded.
Harvest when tops are 1 inch in diameter.
Succession plant every 10 days for continuous harvest.

name: Broccoli
description: A cool-season crop packed with vitamins, producing large green heads and tasty side shoots.
steps: Start indoors 6-8 weeks before last frost.
Transplant 18-24 inches apart when seedlings have 4 leaves.
Keep soil consistently moist.
Side-dress with nitrogen fertilizer midseason.
Harvest center head when buds are tight.
Continue harvesting side shoots for weeks.

name: Onion
description: A kitchen staple that stores well and forms the flavor base of countless dishes worldwide.
steps: Start seeds indoors 8-10 weeks before transplanting.
Plant sets or transplants 1 inch deep, 4-6 inches apart.
Keep soil moist during bulb formation.
Stop watering when tops begin to fall over.
Harvest when tops yellow and fall.
Cure in dry, airy location for 2-3 weeks.

name: Sweet Potato
description: A warm-season root crop that produces nutritious, sweet tubers with beautiful trailing vines.
steps: Grow slips from a sweet potato in water 6 weeks before planting.
Plant slips 4 inches deep after last frost.
Space 12-18 inches apart in raised rows.
Water regularly for first month, then reduce.
Harvest before first frost when leaves yellow.
Cure tubers at 80-85°F for 10 days.

name: Corn
description: A tall, rewarding crop best grown in blocks for proper pollination. Fresh corn is unbeatable.
steps: Sow 1.5 inches deep after last frost when soil is 60°F+.
Plant in blocks of at least 4 rows for wind pollination.
Space 10-12 inches apart, rows 30-36 inches apart.
Water deeply during tasseling and silking.
Side-dress with nitrogen when knee-high.
Harvest when silks turn brown and kernels are milky.

name: Chili Pepper
description: Hot peppers in many varieties, from mild jalapeño to fiery habanero. Heat lovers.
steps: Start seeds indoors 8-10 weeks before last frost.
Plant 0.25 inch deep; keep warm (80-85°F) for germination.
Transplant after night temps stay above 55°F.
Space 18-24 inches apart.
Water consistently but avoid soggy soil.
Harvest at any stage; riper peppers are hotter.

name: Pumpkin
description: A festive fall crop that produces large, orange fruits for carving, baking, and decorating.
steps: Sow 1 inch deep after last frost in warm soil.
Plant in hills, 2-3 seeds per hill, 60-96 inches apart.
Water deeply at the base.
Pinch off excess flowers for larger pumpkins.
Place cardboard under fruits to prevent rot.
Harvest when skin is hard and stem begins to dry.

name: Dill
description: A feathery herb with a bright, tangy flavor, essential for pickles and fish dishes.
steps: Sow 0.25 inch deep directly outdoors after last frost.
Space 12-18 inches apart.
Keep soil moist until established.
Harvest leaves when plant is 8 inches tall.
Allow some plants to flower for seeds.
Self-seeds readily for next season.

name: Parsley
description: A versatile biennial herb used as a garnish and flavor base in cuisines worldwide.
steps: Soak seeds overnight to speed germination.
Sow 0.25 inch deep; parsley is slow to germinate.
Keep soil consistently moist.
Thin to 6-8 inches apart.
Harvest outer stems first, leaving center to grow.
Plants will bolt in year two.

name: Eggplant
description: A heat-loving crop producing glossy purple fruits, staple in Mediterranean and Asian cuisine.
steps: Start seeds indoors 8-10 weeks before last frost.
Keep at 75-85°F for germination.
Transplant when night temps stay above 55°F.
Space 18-24 inches apart.
Stake plants when they reach 12 inches.
Harvest when skin is glossy and firm.

name: Green Bean
description: A productive warm-season crop, available as bush or pole varieties. Easy and rewarding.
steps: Sow 1 inch deep after last frost when soil is 60°F+.
Space bush beans 4-6 inches apart.
Provide trellis for pole varieties.
Water consistently at the base.
Do not handle plants when wet to prevent disease.
Harvest every 2-3 days for continuous production.

name: Celery
description: A crunchy, refreshing vegetable that requires patience and consistent moisture to grow well.
steps: Start seeds indoors 10-12 weeks before last frost.
Press seeds onto surface; they need light to germinate.
Transplant when plants have 3-4 leaves.
Space 8-10 inches apart in rich, moist soil.
Water daily; celery needs constant moisture.
Blanch stalks by mounding soil around them.
Harvest when stalks are 8 inches tall.

name: Cherry Tomato
description: Prolific producers of small, sweet tomatoes perfect for snacking and salads.
steps: Start seeds indoors 6-8 weeks before last frost.
Plant 0.25 inch deep in warm seed-starting mix.
Transplant when soil is 60°F+.
Space 24-36 inches apart with stakes or cages.
Water deeply at base 2-3 times per week.
Harvest when fruits are fully colored and slightly soft.

name: Butternut Squash
description: A sweet, nutty winter squash that stores for months. Easy to grow with plenty of space.
steps: Sow 1 inch deep after last frost in warm soil.
Space 36-60 inches apart.
Water deeply and consistently.
Mulch to retain moisture and suppress weeds.
Harvest when skin is hard and stem is dry.
Cure in sun for 10 days before storing.

name: Pansy
description: Cheerful, colorful flowers with 'face' markings that thrive in cool weather.
steps: Start seeds indoors 8-10 weeks before transplanting.
Press seeds lightly into soil; cover lightly.
Keep in cool, dark location until germination.
Transplant 6-9 inches apart in cool weather.
Deadhead spent flowers regularly.
Replace when summer heat arrives.

name: Dahlia
description: Stunning, showy flowers in endless colors and forms, from dinner-plate to pompon styles.
steps: Plant tubers 4-6 inches deep after last frost.
Space 18-24 inches apart.
Stake tall varieties at planting time.
Water deeply once shoots emerge.
Pinch center bud for bushier plants.
Dig up tubers after first frost in cold climates.

name: Zinnia
description: Vibrant, long-blooming annuals that attract butterflies and make excellent cut flowers.
steps: Sow 0.25 inch deep after last frost.
Space 4-24 inches apart depending on variety.
Water at the base to prevent powdery mildew.
Deadhead spent blooms for continuous flowering.
Cut flowers when outer petals open fully.
Great for butterfly gardens.

name: Petunia
description: Colorful, trumpet-shaped flowers ideal for containers, hanging baskets, and borders.
steps: Press tiny seeds onto moist soil surface.
Keep at 70-75°F; seeds need light to germinate.
Transplant after last frost, 12 inches apart.
Pinch back leggy stems for bushier growth.
Water regularly; petunias are heavy feeders.
Deadhead for continuous blooms all summer.

name: Lemon Tree
description: A beautiful evergreen tree that produces fragrant flowers and tangy yellow fruits year-round.
steps: Purchase a grafted nursery tree for faster fruiting.
Plant in a sunny, sheltered location.
Dig hole twice the root ball width.
Water deeply but let soil dry between waterings.
Fertilize monthly during growing season.
Protect from frost; grow in containers in cold climates.
Harvest when fruits are fully yellow and slightly soft.

name: Grape
description: A rewarding vine crop for fresh eating, juice, and winemaking. Requires training and patience.
steps: Purchase dormant bare-root vines in early spring.
Plant at same depth as nursery, 6-8 feet apart.
Install trellis or arbor system.
Water deeply weekly during first year.
Prune heavily in late winter for best fruit production.
Thin fruit clusters for larger grapes.
Harvest when berries are sweet and easily pull from stem.

name: Beet
description: A dual-purpose root crop — eat the sweet roots roasted and the nutritious greens in salads.
steps: Sow seeds 0.5 inch deep directly outdoors.
Soak seeds overnight for faster germination.
Thin to 3-4 inches apart when 2 inches tall.
Keep soil consistently moist.
Harvest when roots are 1.5-3 inches in diameter.
Don't waste the greens — they're delicious!

name: Asparagus
description: A perennial delicacy that produces tender spears for 20+ years once established.
steps: Purchase 1-year-old crowns for best results.
Dig trenches 6-8 inches deep, 18 inches apart.
Spread roots over soil mound in trench.
Cover gradually as spears grow.
Do NOT harvest for first 2 years.
Harvest spears in year 3 when 6-8 inches tall.
Stop harvesting when spears thin to pencil width.
Let ferns grow through summer to feed roots.

name: Cantaloupe
description: A sweet, aromatic summer melon with orange flesh. Loves heat and long growing seasons.
steps: Start indoors 3-4 weeks before last frost.
Plant 1 inch deep in warm soil (70°F+).
Space 36-48 inches apart in hills.
Water deeply at the base.
Reduce watering as fruits ripen.
Harvest when stem slips easily from fruit.
The melon should smell sweet at the blossom end.

name: Turnip
description: A fast-growing cool-season root crop with edible greens. Sweet when harvested young.
steps: Sow 0.5 inch deep directly outdoors.
Thin to 4-6 inches apart.
Keep soil consistently moist.
Harvest roots at 2-3 inches for sweetest flavor.
Greens can be harvested anytime.
Plant in fall for a sweeter crop.
